steps.explanation: The bacteria doubles every day, so after \(t\) days the quantity is multiplied by \(2^t\). Exponential growth takes the form \(y = a \cdot b^t\) where \(a\) is the initial amount and \(b\) is the daily multiplier.

• Choice A: \(y = \frac{1}{2}(72{,}000)t\) — linear, and halves the initial count. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: \(y = 2(72{,}000)t\) — linear, not exponential. It coincides with the correct value at \(t=1\) (144,000) and \(t=2\) (288,000), but diverges at \(t=3\): linear gives 432,000 while the correct exponential gives \(72{,}000 \cdot 2^3 = 576{,}000\). Eliminated.
  • Choice C: \(y = 72{,}000\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^t\) — exponential decay (shrinks each day). Eliminated.
  • Choice D: \(y = 72{,}000(2)^t\) — exponential growth doubling each day. That matches choice D — D is correct.
